channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: The Future of AI: Anthropic Unveils Updated Models with Autonomous Computer Tasks
item.description: Anthropic releases updated AI models with computer use feature, poised to revolutionize software development and consumer interactions
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: ABBA, Radiohead and The Cure musicians sign AI protest letter against 'unlicensed use' of works
item.description: Musicians from ABBA, Radiohead and The Cure have joined actors and authors in signing a protest letter against the mining of their artistry to build artificial intelligence tools.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Will LLMs become the ultimate mediators for better and for worse? DeepMind researchers and Reddit users seem to agree on that
item.description: Researchers at Google DeepMind have explored the use of AI to mediate political disputes - and the results were surprising.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: How the Army makes software a front and center focus
item.description: "One of the things that we really change as part of the software implementation is, no longer is the Army going to retest everything," said Jennifer Swanson. The post How the Army makes software a front and center focus first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: You’re going to have a robot coworker sooner than you think
item.description: They marched in line, interacted with guests, and poured drinks while making small talk. The vision of the future Elon Musk put forward with his Optimus humanoid robots at Tesla’s recent Cybercab event earlier this month was as astounding as it was unbelievable.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: Meta again testing facial recognition software to fight 'celeb bait' scams
item.description: Meta, the parent company of Facebook and Instagram, is testing facial recognition software to combat “celeb bait” scams on its platforms, as well as to allow users to more easily regain access to their accounts.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Giant fish thought to be extinct is spotted in the Mekong River
item.description: Scientists say a giant fish in the Mekong River thought to be extinct has been spotted three times in recent years.
item.dc:creator: Christina Larson, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: The big, gaping hole in software supply chain security
item.description: If software supply chains consisted solely of open source code, securing them would be easy. Effective tools and methodologies exist for discovering and remediating software supply chain security risks that arise from open source components.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Don’t panic. Here’s how to find your photos after iOS 18 update
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson has the details on the iOS 18 Photos app: Revamped layout, customizable collections and easier photo organization.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Perplexity is sued by Dow Jones and ‘New York Post’ for copyright infringement
item.description: Rupert Murdoch’s Dow Jones, which is the parent company of the Wall Street Journal, and the New York Post both sued AI search startup Perplexity on Monday for infringing copyrighted content. The News Corp.-owned companies accused Perplexity of a “massive amount of illegal copying” of their work.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: FTC's rule banning fake online reviews goes into effect
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal rule banning fake online reviews is now in effect. The Federal Trade Commission issued the rule in August banning the sale or purchase of online reviews.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Gartner: Top 10 strategic technology trends for 2025
item.description: Gartner is sharing its annual look at the hot technologies enterprise customers should be watching out for in the coming years.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Miss having a dedicated work phone? Here’s how to configure your iPhone to separate business from personal use
item.description: Smartphones have enabled us to attend to work from nearly anywhere. But their productivity power also frequently encroaches on our personal lives—making it too easy to reply to business emails or Slack messages when we should be focused on dinner with the fam.
item.dc:creator: Michael Grothaus, Fast Company

item.title: Phone bans in class wanted, but not all-day restrictions: Poll
item.description: 68% of U.S. adults said in a Pew Research Center survey conducted Sept. 30-Oct. 6, 2024 say they approve of such rules.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation

item.title: Rising crypto scams leave Americans reeling from billions in losses
item.description: Scammers have illustrated that cryptocurrency is not as foolproof as it has promised. Americans lost $5.6 billion to crypto scams in 2023, according to the FBI.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: October is Cybersecurity Awareness Month. Here's how to stay safe from scams
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — October is Cybersecurity Awareness Month, which means it's the perfect time to learn how to protect yourself from scams. “Scams have become so sophisticated now.
item.dc:creator: ADRIANA MORGA, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Chinese humanoid robot could be the future of affordable in-home care
item.description: A humanoid robot made by a Chinese company proposes to redefine personalized elderly care and assistance for people with disabilities.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US to probe Tesla's 'Full Self-Driving' system after pedestrian killed in low visibility conditions
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — The U.S. government's road safety agency is investigating Tesla's “Full Self-Driving” system after getting reports of crashes in low-visibility conditions, including one that killed a pedestrian.
item.dc:creator: TOM KRISHER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Massachusetts judge rules Meta must face state's social media lawsuit
item.description: A Massachusetts Superior Court judge has ruled Meta must face a state lawsuit, alleging the company knowingly designed and deployed features harmful to young online users.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Judge delays order in antitrust case requiring Google to open up its app store
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — A federal judge on Friday delayed an order requiring Google to open up its Android app store to more competition until an appeals court decides whether to block the shake-up because of legal questions surrounding a jury's verdict that branded Google as an illegal monopolist.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: DoD ‘fine tunes’ final CMMC program rule, industry turns attention to implementation
item.description: The Pentagon made some key tweaks to the final CMMC program rule. Several program watchers said the changes should help simplify a complex compliance program. The post DoD ‘fine tunes’ final CMMC program rule, industry turns attention to implementation first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: 6 key mobile and IoT/OT attack trend findings
item.description: The role of the CISO continues to expand, driven by the rising number of breaches and cyberattacks like ransomware, as well as SEC requirements for public organizations to disclose material breaches.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Tesla software under investigation after collisions
item.description: The National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA) opened an investigation into Tesla’s Full Self-Driving (FSD) software on Thursday after the system was reportedly involved in four crashes, including one that killed a pedestrian.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Augmented reality screens are a feature, not a product
item.description: Apple released a claustrophobic submarine movie for Apple Vision Pro earlier this month, and it gave me a sinking feeling. The movie Submerged was neither augmented reality (AR) nor virtual reality (VR) nor 360-degree video. It was more like 180-degree video.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Alabama man arrested in SEC social media account hack that led the price of bitcoin to spike
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — An Alabama man was arrested Thursday for his alleged role in the January hack of a U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission social media account that led the price of bitcoin to spike, the Justice Department said. Eric Council Jr.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Treasury kept $4B away from fraudsters this year. It plans to triple that by 2029
item.description: Department officials are building on these fraud-prevention efforts, and expect to prevent $12 billion in improper payments annually by 2029. The post Treasury kept $4B away from fraudsters this year. It plans to triple that by 2029 first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jory Heckman, Federal News Network

item.title: HPE, Dell launch another round of AI servers
item.description: The AI spin continues, with Hewlett Packard Enterprise and Dell Technologies both introducing new servers oriented towards training large language models (LLM).
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Alabama man arrested for hacking Securities and Exchange Commission X account
item.description: Following the unauthorized announcement made during the hack, the DOJ said the price of bitcoin increased by more than $1,000 per bitcoin.
item.dc:creator: Logan Sparkman, NewsNation

item.title: Hit the lights: Mets outfit Citi Field for electric light shows
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The Mets are lighting up Citi Field, even before their batters step up to the plate. About 70 front-office staffers scattered around the ballpark for over five hours to install 3,500 strips of LED lights on each railing ahead of New York's first home postseason game.
item.dc:creator: RONALD BLUM, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: iOS 18: Maximize your privacy by turning off these iPhone settings now
item.description: Tech guru Kurt "CyberGuy" Knutsson reveals some of the iPhone settings you may want to turn off to improve privacy after updating to iOS 18.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How to use Task.WhenEach in .NET 9
item.description: The upcoming release of .NET 9 introduces the Task.WhenEach method, which enables developers to handle asynchronous tasks more elegantly. The Task.WhenEach method allows us to iterate through tasks as they complete, without having to call Task.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: US agency adopts rule to make it easier for consumers to cancel unwanted subscriptions
item.description: The Federal Trade Commission adopted a final rule Wednesday that will require businesses to make it easy for consumers to cancel unwanted subscriptions and memberships.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Survey: 71% of women in IT work long hours to climb ladder
item.description: Nearly three-fourths of women in IT said they work longer hours to improve their chances of career advancement, due in part to gender bias and discrimination.
item.dc:creator: Network World
